shortAnswer: To apply for NASA SBIR Space Tech Grants 2026-2027, start by reviewing the eligibility criteria and preparing a project proposal. Complete 2026-2027 guide to NASA SBIR/STTR grants for space tech startups. Phase I up to $150K, Phase II up to $850K for satellites, remote sensing, propulsion, aeronautics innovation. Funding available: up to $150K (with related programs offering $850K).
shortAnswerQuestion: How can my business apply for NASA SBIR Space Tech Grants 2026-2027 in 2026?

faq.question: Do I have to build a rocket to get a NASA grant?
faq.answer: No. NASA funds a massive array of auxiliary technologies, including robotics, advanced materials, health tech, software algorithms, and life support systems that have both space and earth-bound (commercial) applications.

faq.question: What is a Phase I NASA SBIR award?
faq.answer: Phase I awards are feasibility and concept studies. They typically provide up to $150,000 for a 6-month period to prove the scientific and technical merit of an idea.

faq.question: Who owns the IP developed under a NASA SBIR?
faq.answer: You do. Under the Bayh-Dole Act, small businesses retain the intellectual property rights to technologies developed using SBIR/STTR funding, though the government retains a royalty-free license for federal use.

expertTip.title: NASA Subtopics
expertTip.content: NASA SBIR has very specific subtopics. Your proposal MUST align precisely with an open subtopic—generic 'space tech' proposals will be rejected.
